questions.question: What is a binary semaphore commonly called when used strictly as a locking mechanism?
answers: Condition Variable
Critical Section
Mutex
Monitor

questions.question: What is a critical section inside multi-threaded application software?
answers: A block of code that accesses shared resources and must not be concurrently run
A critical database constraint tracking primary indexing relationships
A background process handling system initialization routines
A protected kernel execution memory register row

questions.question: What classical algorithm can be utilized to safely avoid deadlocks in resource allocation?
answers: Dijkstra's Shortest Path
Round Robin Algorithm
De Morgan's Optimization
Banker's Algorithm

questions.question: What is the role of a Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)?
answers: To act as a specialized cache for virtual-to-physical address translations
To map file allocation records sequentially on disk
To handle network data package buffering streams
To store execution metrics logs for background tasks

questions.question: What state does a system enter when it spends more time swapping pages than executing instructions?
answers: Starvation
Fragmenting
Paging
Thrashing

questions.question: What is the main difference between internal and external fragmentation?
answers: Internal is fixed by defragmentation; external requires virtual memory flushes
External happens inside kernel space; internal happens in user space
External happens on hard drives; internal happens inside CPU registers
Internal occurs inside fixed allocated blocks; external occurs between variable blocks

questions.question: What characterizes a monolithic kernel architecture?
answers: It eliminates context switching overhead entirely
It runs exclusively on virtual machines
OS services are moved to separate user-space modules
All operating system services run inside a single privileged kernel space

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a system call?
answers: To clear CPU cache configurations automatically
To compile source code files into machine code
To manage graphical window rendering pipelines
To provide an interface for user programs to request services from the kernel

questions.question: What is a major advantage of a microkernel compared to a monolithic design?
answers: Smaller total storage size footprint
Higher system stability and isolation of corrupted drivers
Faster execution performance speeds
Lower context switching overhead costs

questions.question: What privilege layer does the operating system kernel run in on modern CPUs?
answers: Kernel mode (Ring 0)
Safe mode
User mode (Ring 3)
Hypervisor mode
